stages.name: Frame
stages.instruction: Frame the task for later work. Identify the core question, constraints, what would make an answer useful, and traps that could lead to premature conclusions.

stages.name: Generate
stages.instruction: Generate plausible approaches, hypotheses, designs, or solution paths. Keep them grounded in the task and make clear why each path might matter.

stages.name: Stress-test
stages.instruction: Stress-test the prior work. Identify failure modes, missing assumptions, alternative explanations, risks, and what evidence would change the recommendation.

stages.name: Synthesize
stages.instruction: Produce only the requested final artifact. Do not mention stage names, prior outputs, task IDs, or that this is an evaluation. Do not include transcript-like framing. Use the prior stages only as working context for a coherent final deliverable.
